content_en:
When a love of this nature is truly internalized, it becomes natural for the person to emulate and imitate his object of admiration. One see this phenomenon in all walks of life. This explains why fans, for example, want to know so many details about the actors, athletes or stars that they idolize. Part of the goal is to know about them and part of the goal is to try to emulate them as much as possible. This feeling of devotion and willingness to emulate must be even greater for the person who realizes that via emulation and following of the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) he can conquer the true way to spiritual purification.

In sum, in order to truly purify one's soul, one must make sure that one's attitude toward the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) is correct. One must have belief in the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and that belief must be correct. One must also

364 Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. 365 Islahi, pp. 95-96. 222

obey the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him), seek to emulate him and love him and his way.366 When all of these matters are fulfilled, one is on the firmest path leading to the purification of the soul, which in itself was one of the main purposes for the sending of the Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him).

Murad offers one more interesting point concerning the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and his role in the purification of one's soul. After discussing the materialism of Western societies, how such societies are even creeping upon the Muslim world and how the West's ultimate emphasis upon what can be materially measured and denial of the unseen is the antithesis of Islam, he noted,

Once you have chosen to live in a "Western type" society, the only source of light for you is the Messenger of Allah. He was also faced with an almost similar situation. As he came down from the cave of Hira', after his experience of receiving the light of Divine guidance, he re-entered a culture and society which were quite "alien" to his Message. His Message began by linking the whole of life to the name of Allah. That was the starting point. All knowledge, all culture, all civilisation and all human action must be centred on one pivot and that is the name of Allah. This was a totally strange Message for the society in which he had to operate. So, we need to look at the Prophet's Sunnah in the context of operating in an "alien" society and see how we can practise a genuine Islamic culture…

Secondly, while living in an "alien" culture, you have to preserve your Islamic identity-not only through rational arguments, but through emotional, cultural and civilisational symbols. It is only the Sunnah that can provide these emotional and civilisational symbols through which you will not only preserve your identity but strengthen and advance it.367

366 Islahi (p. 96) noted, "Obedience without love is hypocrisy and love without obedience and pursuance [that is, emulation] is innovation." 367 Murad, pp. 91-93. 223

In numerous places in the Quran, Allah makes it clear that the key to salvation is not a mere declaration of one's faith or a false faith that has no righteous deeds as its fruits. Instead, the key is a true faith that combines with and is the driving force behind performing righteous deeds. In fact, the scholars note that faith is actually comprised of the belief in the heart, the statement of the tongue and the deeds of the physical body. Hence, a person's faith cannot be complete without performing the proper deeds.

Allah clearly relates the performance of good deeds with having faith. For example, He says, "Whoever works any act of righteousness and has faith, his endeavor will not be rejected: We shall record it in his favor" (21:94). Indeed, the only way to be saved from perdition is through faith and good deeds, as Allah says, "By (the token of) Time (through the ages), verily man is in loss, except those who have faith, do righteous deeds, (join together) in the mutual teaching of truth, and of patience and constancy" (103:1-3). In another verse, Allah clearly shows that it is faith and deeds that lead one to Paradise or the Hell-fire. Allah says, "Nay, whoever earns evil and his sin has surrounded him, they are Companions of the Fire: therein shall they abide (forever). But those who have faith and work righteousness, they are companions of the garden: therein shall they abide (forever)" (2:81-82).368

Hence, the second obligatory step in purifying the soul and becoming beloved to Allah is the performance of the deeds that Allah has made obligatory upon the believers. (This goes hand in hand with the increase in imaan and tauheed and there is a very important dynamic relationship between the two that is difficult sometimes to fathom but which is very clear to the person who experiences it.) Again, this point is made very clearly in the hadith just quoted 368 This does not mean to imply that it is by deeds alone that one is entered into Paradise. Indeed, the greatest deeds that one can perform are not such that they would deserve Paradise in return. However, via the performance of good deeds, Allah showers the person with His grace and mercy. It is this grace and mercy that allows the person to be entered into Paradise. If a person does not have faith followed up with righteous deeds, he is not deserving of Allah's mercy and, hence, he will not receive this great reward from Allah. This is the meaning of the Prophet's words, "One's deeds will not enter anyone into Paradise." They said, "Not even you, O Messenger of Allah?" he replied, "No, not even me unless Allah covers me with His grace and mercy." (Recorded by alBukhari with this wording.) 224

above. In that hadith, Allah has said, "My servant does not draw near to Me with anything more loved by Me than the religious duties I have imposed upon him."

Note that the stress here has been on the obligatory deeds. This is due to their extreme importance. Indeed, everyone should dedicate himself to performing those deeds first. In other words, one has to tend to the obligatory deeds before preoccupying oneself with voluntary deeds. As al-Toofi stated,

The order to perform the obligatory duties is a strict one. The one who does not perform them falls into punishment. On both of these matters, the case of the voluntary deeds is different. They are similar to the obligatory deeds in that in both of them one earns a reward. However, the obligatory deeds are more complete. For that reason, they are more beloved to Allah and take one closer to Him. The obligatory deeds are like the foundation while the voluntary deeds are like the branches and the building. If a person fulfills the obligatory deeds in the way that they are commanded to be fulfilled, with proper respect and esteem by submitting to Him and demonstrating the greatness of His Lordship and submission of His worship, one gets closer to Him in the greatest way.369

The obligatory deeds are like the foundation or roots while the voluntary deeds are like the branches. If a person first fulfills those foundations, he is demonstrating his foundation of being willing to submit to Allah. Hence, that is the best way that he earns his Lord's approval.370However, if one does not first fulfill those deeds that Allah has made obligatory, one does not demonstrate his willingness to submit and obey whatever Allah has ordered. In other words, he fails to fulfill one of the greatest steps in the process of purification. Therefore, the first step must be the fulfillment of the obligatory duties.

The Obligatory Deeds May Be Divided Into The Following Four Categories:

(1) The obligatory deeds (feelings and emotions) of the heart: 369 Quoted in Ahmad Fareed, Al-Tazkiah baina Ahl al-Sunnah wa al-Soofiyah (no publication informa on given), p. 22. 370 Cf., Ahmad ibn Hajar, Fath al-Baari bi-Sharh sahih al-Bukhaari (Makkah: al-Maktaba alTijaariyyah, 1993), vol. 13, p. 145. 225

(2) The ritual pillars of Islam:

(3) The other obligatory acts:

(4) Abstaining from the forbidden acts:

If a person fulfills only the obligatory acts in their proper fashion, he should be able to reach a certain level of spiritual purification. However, most likely, the process of purification will not stop at the obligatory deeds alone. The feeling and love for more purifying acts will flow and the believer will seek other righteous deeds that will bring him even closer to Allah. Hence, the third step in the purification of one's soul is the performance of the voluntary deeds after one has attended to the deeds that are obligatory upon him.

The voluntary deeds include all of the deeds that are not strictly obligated and which have some sign that they are praiseworthy acts. These deeds are also at different levels of virtue. Some virtuous deeds were greatly emphasized by the Prophet (peace be upon him) while others were not so emphasized. In other words, some voluntary deeds are much more virtuous than others. The more virtuous a voluntary deed, the closer it takes a person to Allah.

In referring to the very important hadith qudsi referred to herein more than once, in which the Allah has stated, "My servant does not draw near to Me with anything more beloved to Me than the religious duties that I have imposed upon him; and My servant continues to draw near to Me with supererogatory works such that I love him."371

Finally, it is very important to note-and it is a great blessing from Allah-that this path is not a path that necessarily takes a long amount of time or requires that the person pass through certain stages. A person can become one of the purified and devoted servants of Allah very quickly by sincerity and devotion to Allah. This true sincerity and devotion may come after he performs only a few deeds. Indeed, from the outset, he may perform those deeds that are obligatory upon him and he becomes beloved to Allah. Then he continues

371 Recorded by al-Bukhari. 226

on that path, with Allah guiding him to what is good and proper. This will be a sign that he is continuing as one of Allah's auliyaa (devoted servants). Again, this "easy path" is part of the great mercy and blessings of Allah. 372

A conclusion from the hadith above is that some believers move even closer to Allah by not only fulfilling the obligatory duties but by additionally performing the voluntary deeds (and these are numerous). When a person performs the obligatory deeds, he demonstrates his willingness to submit to Allah. In addition, he is doing what he needs to do to protect himself from Allah's punishment. However, if on top of those deeds he also performs voluntary deeds, this demonstrates his sincerity to Allah and his true willingness to please Allah. This is no longer a matter of fulfilling a command from Allah or rescuing oneself from punishment. Now one is doing the acts to get even closer and become more beloved to Allah.

Therefore, it is no wonder that such people who perform the voluntary deeds (which includes staying away from the disliked deeds) receive a special love from Allah in both this life and in the Hereafter. Allah says about such a servant in the hadith quoted earlier, "My servant continues to draw near to Me with supererogatory works such that I love him. And when I love him, I am his hearing with which he hears, his seeing with which he sees, his hand with which he strikes, and his leg with which he walks. Were he to ask of Me, I would surely give him; and were he to ask Me for refuge, I would surely grant him it."373
content_id:
Ketika cinta semacam ini benar-benar dihayati, menjadi hal yang wajar bagi seseorang untuk meneladani dan meniru sosok yang dikaguminya. Fenomena ini dapat kita saksikan di berbagai aspek kehidupan. Inilah yang menjelaskan mengapa para penggemar, misalnya, ingin mengetahui begitu banyak detail tentang aktor, atlet, atau bintang yang mereka idolakan. Sebagian dari tujuan tersebut adalah untuk mengenal mereka, dan sebagian lagi adalah untuk berusaha meneladani mereka semaksimal mungkin. Perasaan pengabdian dan kesediaan untuk meneladani ini haruslah jauh lebih besar bagi seseorang yang menyadari bahwa melalui peneladanan dan mengikuti Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), ia dapat menaklukkan jalan sejati menuju penyucian jiwa.

Kesimpulannya, untuk benar-benar menyucikan jiwa, seseorang harus memastikan bahwa sikapnya terhadap Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) adalah benar. Seseorang harus beriman kepada Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) dan keimanan itu haruslah tepat. Seseorang juga harus

364 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim. 365 Islahi, hlm. 95-96. 222

menaati Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), berusaha meneladaninya, serta mencintainya dan jalannya.366 Ketika semua hal ini terpenuhi, seseorang berada di jalan yang paling kokoh menuju penyucian jiwa, yang itu sendiri merupakan salah satu tujuan utama diutusnya Nabi Muhammad (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam).

Murad menawarkan satu poin menarik lagi mengenai Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) dan perannya dalam penyucian jiwa. Setelah membahas materialisme masyarakat Barat, bagaimana masyarakat semacam itu bahkan mulai merambah dunia Muslim, dan bagaimana penekanan utama Barat pada hal-hal yang dapat diukur secara material serta pengingkaran terhadap yang gaib merupakan antitesis dari Islam, ia mencatat,

Begitu Anda memilih untuk hidup dalam masyarakat "bergaya Barat", satu-satunya sumber cahaya bagi Anda adalah Rasulullah. Beliau pun pernah menghadapi situasi yang hampir serupa. Saat turun dari gua Hira', setelah pengalaman menerima cahaya petunjuk Ilahi, beliau kembali memasuki budaya dan masyarakat yang cukup "asing" bagi Pesannya. Pesannya dimulai dengan mengaitkan seluruh kehidupan dengan nama Allah. Itulah titik tolaknya. Semua ilmu pengetahuan, semua budaya, semua peradaban, dan semua tindakan manusia harus berpusat pada satu poros, yaitu nama Allah. Ini adalah Pesan yang sama sekali asing bagi masyarakat tempat beliau harus bergerak. Maka kita perlu menelaah Sunnah Nabi dalam konteks bergerak di dalam masyarakat yang "asing" dan melihat bagaimana kita dapat mempraktikkan budaya Islam yang sejati…

Kedua, sementara hidup dalam budaya yang "asing", Anda harus menjaga identitas Islam Anda — bukan hanya melalui argumen rasional, tetapi melalui simbol-simbol emosional, budaya, dan peradaban. Hanya Sunnahlah yang dapat menyediakan simbol-simbol emosional dan peradaban ini, yang melaluinya Anda tidak hanya akan menjaga identitas, tetapi juga memperkuat dan memajukannya.367

366 Islahi (hlm. 96) mencatat, "Ketaatan tanpa cinta adalah kemunafikan, dan cinta tanpa ketaatan serta peneladanan adalah bid'ah." 367 Murad, hlm. 91-93. 223

Di banyak tempat dalam Al-Qur'an, Allah menjelaskan bahwa kunci keselamatan bukanlah sekadar pernyataan keimanan atau keimanan palsu yang tidak menghasilkan amal saleh. Sebaliknya, kuncinya adalah keimanan sejati yang berpadu dengan dan menjadi kekuatan pendorong di balik pelaksanaan amal saleh. Bahkan, para ulama mencatat bahwa keimanan sebenarnya terdiri dari keyakinan dalam hati, pengucapan dengan lisan, dan perbuatan dengan anggota badan. Oleh karena itu, keimanan seseorang tidak dapat sempurna tanpa melaksanakan amal yang semestinya.

Allah secara jelas mengaitkan pelaksanaan amal baik dengan keimanan. Misalnya, Dia berfirman, "Barangsiapa mengerjakan amal-amal saleh, baik laki-laki maupun wanita sedang ia orang yang beriman, maka mereka itu masuk ke dalam surga dan mereka tidak dianiaya walau sedikit pun" (21:94). Memang, satu-satunya cara untuk selamat dari kebinasaan adalah melalui keimanan dan amal saleh, sebagaimana Allah berfirman, "Demi masa. Sesungguhnya manusia itu benar-benar dalam kerugian, kecuali orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal saleh dan nasehat menasehati supaya mentaati kebenaran dan nasehat menasehati supaya menetapi kesabaran" (103:1-3). Dalam ayat lain, Allah secara jelas menunjukkan bahwa keimanan dan amal itulah yang mengantar seseorang ke surga atau neraka. Allah berfirman, "Bukan demikian, barangsiapa berbuat dosa dan ia telah diliputi oleh dosanya, mereka itulah penghuni neraka, mereka kekal di dalamnya. Dan orang-orang yang beriman serta beramal saleh, mereka itu penghuni surga; mereka kekal di dalamnya" (2:81-82).368

Maka, langkah wajib kedua dalam menyucikan jiwa dan menjadi dicintai Allah adalah pelaksanaan amal-amal yang telah Allah wajibkan atas orang-orang beriman. (Hal ini berjalan seiring dengan peningkatan iman dan tauhid, dan terdapat hubungan dinamis yang sangat penting di antara keduanya yang terkadang sulit dipahami, namun sangat jelas bagi orang yang mengalaminya secara langsung.) Sekali lagi, poin ini dinyatakan dengan sangat jelas dalam hadits yang baru saja dikutip. 368 Ini tidak berarti bahwa hanya dengan amal saja seseorang masuk surga. Sesungguhnya, amal terbaik yang dapat dilakukan seseorang tidak sedemikian rupa sehingga layak mendapat balasan surga. Namun, melalui pelaksanaan amal baik, Allah melimpahkan karunia dan rahmat-Nya kepada orang tersebut. Karunia dan rahmat inilah yang memungkinkan seseorang masuk surga. Jika seseorang tidak memiliki keimanan yang diikuti dengan amal saleh, ia tidak berhak mendapat rahmat Allah, dan karenanya ia tidak akan menerima pahala yang agung ini dari Allah. Inilah makna sabda Nabi, "Amal seseorang tidak akan memasukkannya ke dalam surga." Mereka bertanya, "Tidak juga engkau, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau menjawab, "Tidak, bahkan aku pun tidak, kecuali Allah meliputi aku dengan karunia dan rahmat-Nya." (Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dengan redaksi ini.) 224

di atas. Dalam hadits tersebut, Allah berfirman, "Hamba-Ku tidak mendekat kepada-Ku dengan sesuatu yang lebih Aku cintai daripada kewajiban-kewajiban agama yang telah Aku tetapkan atasnya."

Perlu dicatat bahwa penekanan di sini ada pada amal-amal wajib. Hal ini disebabkan oleh pentingnya yang sangat besar. Sesungguhnya, setiap orang hendaknya mendedikasikan dirinya untuk melaksanakan kewajiban-kewajiban tersebut terlebih dahulu. Dengan kata lain, seseorang harus memperhatikan amal-amal wajib sebelum menyibukkan diri dengan amal-amal sunnah. Sebagaimana al-Thufi menyatakan,

Perintah untuk melaksanakan kewajiban-kewajiban adalah perintah yang tegas. Orang yang tidak melaksanakannya akan mendapat hukuman. Pada kedua hal ini, kondisi amal-amal sunnah berbeda. Amal sunnah serupa dengan amal wajib dalam hal keduanya sama-sama mendatangkan pahala. Namun, amal wajib lebih sempurna. Oleh sebab itu, amal wajib lebih dicintai Allah dan lebih mendekatkan kepada-Nya. Amal wajib bagaikan pondasi, sementara amal sunnah bagaikan cabang dan bangunan. Jika seseorang memenuhi amal wajib sebagaimana diperintahkan, dengan penghormatan dan pengagungan yang semestinya, dengan tunduk kepada-Nya dan menunjukkan kebesaran Rububiyah-Nya serta ketundukan ibadah-Nya, maka ia mendekat kepada-Nya dengan cara yang paling agung.369

Amal wajib bagaikan pondasi atau akar, sementara amal sunnah bagaikan cabang-cabangnya. Jika seseorang terlebih dahulu memenuhi pondasi-pondasi tersebut, ia menunjukkan landasan kesediaannya untuk tunduk kepada Allah. Maka itulah cara terbaik ia meraih ridha Tuhannya.370 Namun, jika seseorang tidak terlebih dahulu memenuhi amal-amal yang telah Allah wajibkan, ia tidak menunjukkan kesediaannya untuk tunduk dan menaati apa pun yang Allah perintahkan. Dengan kata lain, ia gagal memenuhi salah satu langkah terpenting dalam proses penyucian. Oleh karena itu, langkah pertama haruslah pemenuhan kewajiban-kewajiban yang telah ditetapkan.

Amal-Amal Wajib Dapat Dibagi Menjadi Empat Kategori Berikut:

(1) Amal wajib (perasaan dan emosi) hati: 369 Dikutip dalam Ahmad Fareed, Al-Tazkiyah baina Ahl al-Sunnah wa al-Shufiyyah (tanpa informasi penerbitan), hlm. 22. 370 Lihat, Ahmad ibn Hajar, Fath al-Bari bi-Syarh Shahih al-Bukhari (Makkah: al-Maktabah al-Tijariyyah, 1993), jil. 13, hlm. 145. 225

(2) Rukun-rukun ritual Islam:

(3) Amal-amal wajib lainnya:

(4) Menjauhkan diri dari perbuatan-perbuatan yang diharamkan:

Jika seseorang hanya memenuhi amal-amal wajib dengan cara yang semestinya, ia seharusnya mampu mencapai tingkat tertentu dalam penyucian jiwa. Namun, kemungkinan besar, proses penyucian tidak akan berhenti pada amal-amal wajib saja. Perasaan dan kecintaan terhadap amal-amal penyucian yang lebih banyak akan mengalir, dan orang beriman akan mencari amal-amal saleh lain yang akan semakin mendekatkannya kepada Allah. Maka, langkah ketiga dalam penyucian jiwa adalah pelaksanaan amal-amal sunnah setelah seseorang telah menunaikan amal-amal yang wajib atasnya.

Amal-amal sunnah mencakup semua amal yang tidak diwajibkan secara mutlak namun memiliki tanda-tanda bahwa amal tersebut adalah perbuatan yang terpuji. Amal-amal ini juga berada pada tingkatan keutamaan yang berbeda-beda. Sebagian amal sunnah sangat ditekankan oleh Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam), sementara yang lainnya tidak terlalu ditekankan. Dengan kata lain, sebagian amal sunnah jauh lebih utama dari yang lainnya. Semakin utama suatu amal sunnah, semakin dekat pula ia membawa seseorang kepada Allah.

Merujuk pada hadits qudsi yang sangat penting yang telah disebutkan lebih dari sekali dalam tulisan ini, di mana Allah telah berfirman, "Hamba-Ku tidak mendekat kepada-Ku dengan sesuatu yang lebih Aku cintai daripada kewajiban-kewajiban agama yang telah Aku tetapkan atasnya; dan hamba-Ku terus mendekat kepada-Ku dengan amalan-amalan sunnah sehingga Aku mencintainya."371

Akhirnya, sangat penting untuk dicatat—dan ini adalah karunia besar dari Allah—bahwa jalan ini bukanlah jalan yang harus ditempuh dalam waktu yang lama atau mengharuskan seseorang melewati tahapan-tahapan tertentu. Seseorang dapat menjadi salah satu dari hamba-hamba Allah yang suci dan berdedikasi dengan sangat cepat melalui keikhlasan dan pengabdian kepada Allah. Keikhlasan dan pengabdian yang sejati ini mungkin datang setelah ia hanya melakukan beberapa amal saja. Bahkan, sejak awal, ia mungkin menunaikan amal-amal yang diwajibkan atasnya dan ia pun menjadi dicintai oleh Allah. Kemudian ia melanjutkan

371 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari. 226

di jalan itu, dengan Allah membimbingnya kepada apa yang baik dan benar. Ini akan menjadi tanda bahwa ia terus berada dalam golongan auliyaa Allah (hamba-hamba yang berdedikasi). Sekali lagi, "jalan yang mudah" ini merupakan bagian dari rahmat dan karunia Allah yang agung. 372

Kesimpulan dari hadits di atas adalah bahwa sebagian orang beriman bergerak semakin dekat kepada Allah tidak hanya dengan menunaikan kewajiban-kewajiban yang diwajibkan, tetapi juga dengan menjalankan amal-amal sunnah (yang jumlahnya sangat banyak). Ketika seseorang menunaikan amal-amal wajib, ia menunjukkan kesediaannya untuk tunduk kepada Allah. Selain itu, ia melakukan apa yang perlu ia lakukan untuk melindungi dirinya dari azab Allah. Namun, jika di atas amal-amal tersebut ia juga melaksanakan amal-amal sunnah, hal ini menunjukkan keikhlasannya kepada Allah dan kesediaannya yang tulus untuk menyenangkan Allah. Ini bukan lagi sekadar perkara menunaikan perintah Allah atau menyelamatkan diri dari azab. Kini seseorang melakukan amal-amal itu untuk semakin mendekat dan semakin dicintai oleh Allah.

Oleh karena itu, tidak mengherankan apabila orang-orang yang menunaikan amal-amal sunnah (yang mencakup pula menjauhi amal-amal yang dibenci) mendapatkan cinta yang istimewa dari Allah, baik di dunia maupun di akhirat. Allah berfirman tentang hamba seperti itu dalam hadits yang telah dikutip sebelumnya, "Hamba-Ku terus mendekat kepada-Ku dengan amalan-amalan sunnah sehingga Aku mencintainya. Dan apabila Aku mencintainya, Aku menjadi pendengarannya yang dengannya ia mendengar, penglihatannya yang dengannya ia melihat, tangannya yang dengannya ia memukul, dan kakinya yang dengannya ia berjalan. Jika ia meminta kepada-Ku, niscaya Aku akan memberinya; dan jika ia meminta perlindungan kepada-Ku, niscaya Aku akan melindunginya."373
title_id: Terjemahan Part 44 (*Biographies of the Rightly-Guided Caliphs*) telah ditambahkan ke `044.json` — mencakup seluruh kisah pembunuhan 'Umar ibn Al-Khattab radhiyallahu 'anhu oleh Abu Lu'lu'ah, konspirasi di baliknya, dan saat-saat terakhir 'Umar sebelum wafat.
content_ar:
When a love of this nature is truly internalized, it becomes natural for the person to emulate and imitate his object of admiration. One see this phenomenon in all walks of life. This explains why fans, for example, want to know so many details about the actors, athletes or stars that they idolize. Part of the goal is to know about them and part of the goal is to try to emulate them as much as possible. This feeling of devotion and willingness to emulate must be even greater for the person who realizes that via emulation and following of النبي ﷺ (peace and blessings of الله be upon him) he can conquer the true way to spiritual purification.

In sum, in order to truly purify one's soul, one must make sure that one's attitude toward رسول الله ﷺ (peace and blessings of الله be upon him) is correct. One must have belief in رسول الله ﷺ (peace and blessings of الله be upon him) and that belief must be correct. One must also

364 Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. 365 Islahi, pp. 95-96. 222

obey النبي ﷺ (peace and blessings of الله be upon him), seek to emulate him and love him and his way.366 When all of these matters are fulfilled, one is on the firmest path leading to the purification of the soul, which in itself was one of the main purposes for the sending of the النبي محمد ﷺ (peace and blessings of الله be upon him).

Murad offers one more interesting point concerning رسول الله ﷺ (peace and blessings of الله be upon him) and his role in the purification of one's soul. After discussing the materialism of Western societies, how such societies are even creeping upon the Muslim world and how the West's ultimate emphasis upon what can be materially measured and denial of the unseen is the antithesis of Islam, he noted,

Once you have chosen to live in a "Western type" society, the only source of light for you is رسول الله ﷺ. He was also faced with an almost similar situation. As he came down from the cave of Hira', after his experience of receiving the light of Divine guidance, he re-entered a culture and society which were quite "alien" to his Message. His Message began by linking the whole of life to the name of الله. That was the starting point. All knowledge, all culture, all civilisation and all human action must be centred on one pivot and that is the name of الله. This was a totally strange Message for the society in which he had to operate. So, we need to look at النبي ﷺ's السنة النبوية in the context of operating in an "alien" society and see how we can practise a genuine Islamic culture…

Secondly, while living in an "alien" culture, you have to preserve your Islamic identity-not only through rational arguments, but through emotional, cultural and civilisational symbols. It is only the السنة النبوية that can provide these emotional and civilisational symbols through which you will not only preserve your identity but strengthen and advance it.367

366 Islahi (p. 96) noted, "Obedience without love is hypocrisy and love without obedience and pursuance [that is, emulation] is innovation." 367 Murad, pp. 91-93. 223

In numerous places in the القرآن الكريم, الله makes it clear that the key to salvation is not a mere declaration of one's faith or a false faith that has no righteous deeds as its fruits. Instead, the key is a true faith that combines with and is the driving force behind performing righteous deeds. In fact, the scholars note that faith is actually comprised of the belief in the heart, the statement of the tongue and the deeds of the physical body. Hence, a person's faith cannot be complete without performing the proper deeds.

الله clearly relates the performance of good deeds with having faith. For example, He says, "Whoever works any act of righteousness and has faith, his endeavor will not be rejected: We shall record it in his favor" (21:94). Indeed, the only way to be saved from perdition is through faith and good deeds, as الله says, "By (the token of) Time (through the ages), verily man is in loss, except those who have faith, do righteous deeds, (join together) in the mutual teaching of truth, and of patience and constancy" (103:1-3). In another verse, الله clearly shows that it is faith and deeds that lead one to Paradise or the Hell-fire. الله says, "Nay, whoever earns evil and his sin has surrounded him, they are الصحابة of the Fire: therein shall they abide (forever). But those who have faith and work righteousness, they are الصحابة of the garden: therein shall they abide (forever)" (2:81-82).368

Hence, the second obligatory step in purifying the soul and becoming beloved to الله is the performance of the deeds that الله has made obligatory upon the believers. (This goes hand in hand with the increase in imaan and tauheed and there is a very important dynamic relationship between the two that is difficult sometimes to fathom but which is very clear to the person who experiences it.) Again, this point is made very clearly in the الحديث just quoted 368 This does not mean to imply that it is by deeds alone that one is entered into Paradise. Indeed, the greatest deeds that one can perform are not such that they would deserve Paradise in return. However, via the performance of good deeds, الله showers the person with His grace and mercy. It is this grace and mercy that allows the person to be entered into Paradise. If a person does not have faith followed up with righteous deeds, he is not deserving of الله's mercy and, hence, he will not receive this great reward from الله. This is the meaning of النبي ﷺ's words, "One's deeds will not enter anyone into Paradise." They said, "Not even you, O رسول الله ﷺ?" he replied, "No, not even me unless الله covers me with His grace and mercy." (Recorded by alBukhari with this wording.) 224

above. In that الحديث, الله has said, "My servant does not draw near to Me with anything more loved by Me than the religious duties I have imposed upon him."

Note that the stress here has been on the obligatory deeds. This is due to their extreme importance. Indeed, everyone should dedicate himself to performing those deeds first. In other words, one has to tend to the obligatory deeds before preoccupying oneself with voluntary deeds. As al-Toofi stated,

The order to perform the obligatory duties is a strict one. The one who does not perform them falls into punishment. On both of these matters, the case of the voluntary deeds is different. They are similar to the obligatory deeds in that in both of them one earns a reward. However, the obligatory deeds are more complete. For that reason, they are more beloved to الله and take one closer to Him. The obligatory deeds are like the foundation while the voluntary deeds are like the branches and the building. If a person fulfills the obligatory deeds in the way that they are commanded to be fulfilled, with proper respect and esteem by submitting to Him and demonstrating the greatness of His Lordship and submission of His worship, one gets closer to Him in the greatest way.369

The obligatory deeds are like the foundation or roots while the voluntary deeds are like the branches. If a person first fulfills those foundations, he is demonstrating his foundation of being willing to submit to الله. Hence, that is the best way that he earns his Lord's approval.370However, if one does not first fulfill those deeds that الله has made obligatory, one does not demonstrate his willingness to submit and obey whatever الله has ordered. In other words, he fails to fulfill one of the greatest steps in the process of purification. Therefore, the first step must be the fulfillment of the obligatory duties.

The Obligatory Deeds May Be Divided Into The Following Four Categories:

(1) The obligatory deeds (feelings and emotions) of the heart: 369 Quoted in Ahmad Fareed, Al-Tazkiah baina Ahl al-السنة النبوية wa al-Soofiyah (no publication informa on given), p. 22. 370 Cf., Ahmad ibn Hajar, Fath al-Baari bi-Sharh sahih al-Bukhaari (Makkah: al-Maktaba alTijaariyyah, 1993), vol. 13, p. 145. 225

(2) The ritual pillars of Islam:

(3) The other obligatory acts:

(4) Abstaining from the forbidden acts:

If a person fulfills only the obligatory acts in their proper fashion, he should be able to reach a certain level of spiritual purification. However, most likely, the process of purification will not stop at the obligatory deeds alone. The feeling and love for more purifying acts will flow and the believer will seek other righteous deeds that will bring him even closer to الله. Hence, the third step in the purification of one's soul is the performance of the voluntary deeds after one has attended to the deeds that are obligatory upon him.

The voluntary deeds include all of the deeds that are not strictly obligated and which have some sign that they are praiseworthy acts. These deeds are also at different levels of virtue. Some virtuous deeds were greatly emphasized by النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) while others were not so emphasized. In other words, some voluntary deeds are much more virtuous than others. The more virtuous a voluntary deed, the closer it takes a person to الله.

In referring to the very important الحديث qudsi referred to herein more than once, in which the الله has stated, "My servant does not draw near to Me with anything more beloved to Me than the religious duties that I have imposed upon him; and My servant continues to draw near to Me with supererogatory works such that I love him."371

Finally, it is very important to note-and it is a great blessing from الله-that this path is not a path that necessarily takes a long amount of time or requires that the person pass through certain stages. A person can become one of the purified and devoted servants of الله very quickly by sincerity and devotion to الله. This true sincerity and devotion may come after he performs only a few deeds. Indeed, from the outset, he may perform those deeds that are obligatory upon him and he becomes beloved to الله. Then he continues

371 Recorded by al-Bukhari. 226

on that path, with الله guiding him to what is good and proper. This will be a sign that he is continuing as one of الله's auliyaa (devoted servants). Again, this "easy path" is part of the great mercy and blessings of الله. 372

A conclusion from the الحديث above is that some believers move even closer to الله by not only fulfilling the obligatory duties but by additionally performing the voluntary deeds (and these are numerous). When a person performs the obligatory deeds, he demonstrates his willingness to submit to الله. In addition, he is doing what he needs to do to protect himself from الله's punishment. However, if on top of those deeds he also performs voluntary deeds, this demonstrates his sincerity to الله and his true willingness to please الله. This is no longer a matter of fulfilling a command from الله or rescuing oneself from punishment. Now one is doing the acts to get even closer and become more beloved to الله.

Therefore, it is no wonder that such people who perform the voluntary deeds (which includes staying away from the disliked deeds) receive a special love from الله in both this life and in the Hereafter. الله says about such a servant in the الحديث quoted earlier, "My servant continues to draw near to Me with supererogatory works such that I love him. And when I love him, I am his hearing with which he hears, his seeing with which he sees, his hand with which he strikes, and his leg with which he walks. Were he to ask of Me, I would surely give him; and were he to ask Me for refuge, I would surely grant him it."373
